name: tie_qk_coprod_exp034
hypothesis: QUERY-KEY ALIGNMENT via structural tie. exp033 PROVEN: the Kronecker binding FORMS with compaction (ideal key-query retrieves value, cos 0.35) but the ACTUAL answer-position query mismatches the stored key (cos 0.10). The coproduct q/k use SEPARATE projections (W_q_a vs W_k_a); tying them (k_a=q_a, k_b=q_b by computation) removes a degree of freedom so the same input produces matching q and k. The model still has the dense (frozen) q/k untied, but the coproduct (trainable) contribution to q and k is now identical. Hypothesis: this structural symmetry lets CE find query-key alignment faster -> recall emerges. If recall>0 -> alignment is the lever and the tie fixes it. If recall=0 -> the tie alone insufficient (CE still doesn't reward alignment; loss ceiling stands). Direct comparison to exp001 (seq_len=512, untied, tokacc 0.296 recall 0).
